rights_policy: Link-only factual indexing. Do not vendor guide text, photos, video, maps, or social-media content without item-level permission.

stationing_policy.published: Preserve a source-published river mile or kilometer and convert it to meters from the authored put-in.
stationing_policy.ordered_only: Generate a visibly provisional station from published downstream order so the editor can display a marker; replace it with GPS, imagery, or guide-reviewed stationing before terrain or solver promotion.
stationing_policy.no_geometry_claim: A marker records review intent and does not assert exact bank, bed, hazard, or runnable-line geometry.

sources.title: South Fork American River Rafting: Mile-by-Mile
South Fork American River Map
Behind the Scenery: Colorado River Rapids
Hydraulic Maps of Major Rapids, Grand Canyon, Arizona
List of Grand Canyon rapids and features
Pacuare River Map
Pacuare River Whitewater Guide and Map
Zambezi River: Batoka Gorge
Victoria Falls Rafting Rapid Guide
Rio Futaleufu: Terminator Section
Futaleufu FastTrack Rafting
BC Whitewater Chilko-Chilcotin-Fraser Route Notes
sources.evidence_scope: Bidwell Rapid class, scout recognition, and low/medium/high 08MA002 flow-photo context; no copied media or exact geometry.

sources.title: Rafting Chilko River, North America's Most Notorious Rapids
sources.evidence_scope: Published downstream-name evidence for Bidwell, Lava Canyon, White Mile, Green Mile, and Miracle Canyon; no exact stations.

sources.title: American Whitewater Accident Report 86686: Chilko White Mile
sources.evidence_scope: White Mile high-flow flip, midstream-rock, cold-water, and long-swim consequence evidence; historical report is not an exact line or station source.

sources.title: American Whitewater Reach 148: South Fork American, Chili Bar Dam to Coloma
sources.evidence_scope: Meat Grinder right-side standard line, hidden holes, railroad-grade scout/portage trail, and foot-entrapment/flush-drowning history; Troublemaker rocky-island split, Gunsight final drop, Double Trouble alternate right line, and downstream Hazard Rock pourover with foot-entrapment crack.

sources.title: GoRafting South Fork American Rapid Guide: Meat Grinder
sources.evidence_scope: Right-of-center entry, two offset mid-river holes split left-to-right, Death Star Rock rooster-tail flat-wrap hazard, exit wave train with larger middle wave, and flow thresholds (~2,000 cfs wave/lateral transition, ~5,000 cfs Son of Meat Grinder laterals).

sources.title: GoRafting South Fork American Rapid Guide: Troublemaker
sources.evidence_scope: Left-bank scout eddy, tree-island right-channel entry, lead-in and runout center sieve piles, S-bend right lateral, clip-left main-hole move, Peanut Gallery left wall, Snaggle Tooth pyramid rock, right safety eddy, and high-water (~3,500+ cfs) left-of-island and Double Trouble alternatives.

sources.title: American River Resort: Troublemaker!
sources.evidence_scope: Boulder garden into a falls with far-left S channel, low-flow narrow path, right diagonal wave flip hazard, hard-exit left eddy, gut-of-the-hole placement, Gunsight Rock wrap channel, and the advanced eddy-left ferry exit.

sources.title: OARS: The 10 Best Whitewater Rapids on the American River
sources.evidence_scope: Troublemaker zig-zags boater's left to right; the main drop is split in two by Gunsight Rock; rafts bounce off either side of Gunsight or occasionally run directly over the top.

sources.title: Whitewater Guidebook: South Fork of the American River
sources.evidence_scope: Meatgrinder as a long, tricky rapid with big hydraulics; Troublemaker as the most difficult rapid on the South Fork, scouted from river left.

sources.title: River Brain: Meatgrinder (Quarter Mile) Rapid, South Fork American Chili Bar
sources.evidence_scope: Quarter Mile alias, class III-III+ range, long continuous rocky character with most water funneling toward the bottom near the right bank, and an 800-4,000 cfs recommended flow range.

sources.title: River Brain: Troublemaker (S-Turn) Rapid, South Fork American Chili Bar
sources.evidence_scope: S-Turn alias, class III-IV range, two stacked holes (one at the start, one immediately below on the sharp right-hand turn), the squeeze between Gunsight Rock and the left bank, left-bank scouting, and holes growing bigger and more powerful as levels rise.

sources.title: Garden Betty: Troublemaker on the American River (trip report)
sources.evidence_scope: First-person run at 1,600 cfs: powerful diagonal wave, swift-moving hole, big drop next to Gunsight Rock, kayak eddy-left ferry line past Gunsight, and the head-on drop line between two boulders.

rivers.display_name: South Fork American: Chili Bar to Folsom Reservoir

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates III; the mile guide and American Whitewater reach list III+.

relative_position.notes: Lead-in can be run left or far right at some flows; either way work back to center, entering the main rapid right-of-center.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: rock-dominated; the lead-in channels are shallow and the right-of-center entry line matters most (GoRafting low-flow line, below ~2,000 cfs).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: run the lead-in left or far right and ferry back to center before the main tongue (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: above ~2,000 cfs waves and laterals off the right replace rocks as the primary hazard; the lead-in washes out but pushes harder toward the right-side laterals (GoRafting).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: A swim here is long and rocky: the boulder garden runs over a quarter mile (AW map) and the AW reach history records fatal foot entrapments at summer flows.

relative_position.notes: A small diversion canal removes water on the left; a large rock is visible just left of center and marks the main entry about 10 m to its right (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: rock well exposed and easy to identify; the canal draw on the left is proportionally stronger at low water.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: visible left-of-center landmark for the ~10 m entry offset (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: still visible; the entry read shifts to wave features but the rock remains the entry landmark.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Primarily a navigation landmark; drifting left of it puts the raft into the shallow canal-side rocks.

relative_position.notes: Enter the main rapid about 10 m right of the large rock, slanting left-to-right through a slot between submerged rocks (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the slot is the crux; the submerged rocks are barely covered and the left-to-right slant must be precise.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: an easy move, but failing to make it can have severe consequences because the biggest waves and holes are still ahead (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the slot washes toward waves; missing the move feeds the raft into the right-side laterals with flip potential (GoRafting, above ~2,000 cfs).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Blowing the entry sets the raft up badly for the rest of the quarter-mile boulder garden (AW map).

relative_position.notes: Large boulders and hidden holes over a shallow bed for more than a quarter mile; the standard route stays down the right (AW reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: most exposed and most technical; partially exposed or barely covered rocks require continuous maneuvering, and foot-entrapment risk is highest at summer flows (AW reach history).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: rocks barely covered; hidden holes remain the primary hazard (AW reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: rocks cover and the hazard shifts to hydraulics; the AW reach records a flush drowning at high spring flows.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: American Whitewater documents two fatal foot entrapments at summer flows and a flush drowning at high spring flows in this rapid.

relative_position.notes: Two offset holes in the middle of the river; split them moving left to right (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the holes read as rocky pourovers; split them on the left-to-right diagonal per the low-flow line (GoRafting, below ~2,000 cfs).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: both offset holes are formed and must be split on the left-to-right diagonal (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: above ~2,000 cfs the holes wash into bigger waves with laterals off the right side; flip potential replaces the split move (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: Shallow rock throwing a rooster tail below the offset holes; avoid it by moving right (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: barely covered with a prominent rooster tail; the flat-wrap hazard for rafts is at its worst (GoRafting low-flow description).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the rooster tail still marks the rock; move right to clear it (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: deeper cover mutes the rooster tail and the hazard hands off to the right-side laterals (GoRafting, above ~2,000 cfs).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Known to many outfitters as the Death Star Rock; a flat wrap hazard for rafts (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: The wave train and current funnel toward Rhino Rock on the right; most of the water funnels toward the bottom near the right bank (AW map; River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: waves are small and the funnel reads as a rocky channel along the right.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: keep the raft into the waves as the current funnels toward Rhino Rock (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger waves and laterals come off the right side; the large waves have the potential to flip rafts (GoRafting, above ~2,000 cfs).

relative_position.notes: Large rock on the right near the bottom of the rapid where the funneled current converges; wave size decreases below it (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: exposed, with a narrow channel past it.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the current funnels rafts toward it; ride the wave train past on its left (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: partially covered with pushier water driving toward it; contact risk rises with the right-side laterals.

relative_position.notes: After Rhino Rock wave size decreases; eddy out on the left, or finish the rapid along the right (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: well defined and an easy catch.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard regroup eddy after Rhino Rock (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the eddy line is sharper and faster; the catch is harder but it remains the standard regroup.

relative_position.notes: Runout wave train with a larger wave to hit in the middle at the end (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small; mostly rock dodging through the runout.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a fun wave train with a larger middle wave at the end (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: larger; the Son of Meat Grinder rapid below builds right-bank laterals only well above this band (GoRafting notes them above ~5,000 cfs).

relative_position.notes: An excellent trail for scouting and portaging runs on river right along an old railroad grade (AW reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: scout recommended for first-timers; the low-water slot line is easiest to read from the trail.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: full-length right-bank scout and portage option (AW reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: same trail; scouting recommended before committing to the wave-dominated right line.

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates III; the mile guide and AW reach list III+; the AW map and River Brain report up to IV-/IV at flow.

relative_position.notes: Scout from river left; most parties scout (Whitewater Guidebook). Shove off from the scout eddy into the right channel of the tree island (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: scout strongly advised; at lower flows the true path is narrow indeed (AW map).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard river-left scout stop before the drop (Whitewater Guidebook; River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: scout to choose between the standard line and the high-water alternatives that open above ~3,500 cfs (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: The current splits around a rocky island at the lead-in (AW reach); take the right channel of the tree island to follow the deepest flow, then work back left (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the left channel is too shallow; the right channel of the island carries the only reliable water (GoRafting deepest-flow note).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: right channel, then ferry back left to line up the S-bend (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the right channel remains standard; above ~3,500 cfs a left-of-island entry punching two small stacked holes becomes an option (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: Just downstream of the tree island is a sieve pile in the center of the flow (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: most exposed and most dangerous to a swimmer; stay with the deep right-channel flow.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: covered but straining current; keep rafts and swimmers out of the center below the island (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: more water over the pile; still a no-go zone for swimmers.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Sieves strain current through rock; swimmers must avoid the center below the island (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: The rapid zig-zags from boater's left to right (OARS); after the island, enter the S-bend where a small lateral wave off the right side sets up the move onto the main hole (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: at lower flows the true path is narrow indeed (AW map); precise S-turns between exposed rock.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: run at a very fun 1,600 cfs in a June trip report (Garden Betty); clip the main hole on the left with a slight right angle (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the holes get bigger and more powerful as levels rise (River Brain); the S-bend becomes pushier and less forgiving.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Reported class disagrees across sources: III (GoRafting), III+ (mile guide, AW reach), IV- (AW map), III-IV (River Brain).

relative_position.notes: A boulder garden, followed by a falls, with an S channel on the far left (AW map; American River Resort).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: narrow and rocky; the S channel is the technical low-water sneak past the main drop.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: available but tight; most rafts run the main line.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: washes toward the main flow; the documented high-water alternative shifts to the left-of-island stacked-holes entry (GoRafting, above ~3,500 cfs).

relative_position.notes: Shade right below the left-bend entry and a large diagonal wave tends to flip rafts (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: weaker, but the narrow channel makes it harder to stay off it.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a fully formed raft-flipper guarding the right side; a powerful diagonal wave threatening to flip any boat that goes over it (Garden Betty, run at 1,600 cfs).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger and more powerful with rising water (River Brain); highest flip probability in this band.

relative_position.notes: Precise placement into the gut of the hole surfs the raft next to a big rock on the left below (AW map); clip the main hole on the left side with a slight right angle so it redirects the boat right (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: smaller, but the precise-gut placement matters because the surrounding rock is exposed.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a swift-moving hole (Garden Betty); the clip-left, angle-right move is the standard raft line (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: as water levels rise the holes get bigger and more powerful (River Brain).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: River Brain counts two holes - one at the start and one immediately below on the sharp right-hand turn - where other sources describe a single gut hole; disagreement preserved via the separate right-turn hole record.

feature_inventory.display_name: Second hole on the right-hand turn

relative_position.notes: River Brain: a second hole immediately below the first on the sharp right-hand turn; rafts hit both holes while squeezing between Gunsight Rock and the left bank.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: rocky pourover character.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: formed; inflatable kayaks may run the first hole far left and then paddle hard to run right of Gunsight Rock (River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger and more powerful with rising levels (River Brain).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: May be the same feature other sources describe as the right diagonal wave below the left-bend entry; kept separate to preserve the source disagreement.

relative_position.notes: Go too far left and you end up in an eddy that is hard to exit (AW map); advanced boaters eddy left on purpose, then ferry across the hole and exit right of Gunsight Rock (AW map; Garden Betty).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: easy to enter by accident in the narrow channel; the exit ferry is technical.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: kayaks eddied out before ferrying across the currents and gliding past Gunsight Rock (Garden Betty, run at 1,600 cfs).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the eddy line becomes a strong seam that can pull a boat in and spit it back out in seconds (Garden Betty); exiting on line is harder.

relative_position.notes: Left wall below the main hole known as the Peanut Gallery; the clip-left hole move redirects the boat right to avoid it (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: more rock exposed at its base; less push toward the wall.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the main-hole deflection sets the raft away from the wall (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: pushier current drives boats toward the wall if the hole move is missed.

relative_position.notes: The main drop is split in two by Gunsight Rock (OARS); the steep channel runs along it and rafts often end up wrapped (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: maximum exposure; the channel along it is narrowest and wrap risk is highest (AW map low-flow narrow-path note).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: rafts bounce off either side of Gunsight and occasionally go directly over the top (OARS).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: more water over and around the rock; bounce-overs are more common, wraps less frequent but higher-energy.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: GoRafting describes a large pyramid rock named Snaggle Tooth marking a drop to its left and never uses the name Gunsight; recorded as a source-name disagreement for the bottom-drop rock.

relative_position.notes: Gunsight Rock forms the final steep drop (AW reach); a falls following the boulder garden (American River Resort); the big drop to the left of Gunsight is the one to avoid (Garden Betty).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: steepest and most consequential; the kayak line charges the drop head-on, aligned between two big boulders (Garden Betty).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a clean drop between the two channels split by Gunsight (OARS; Garden Betty ran it at 1,600 cfs).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the drop smooths into a bigger hydraulic; power over precision.

relative_position.notes: Alternate right line, steep and turbulent with a high likelihood of hitting the right wall (AW reach); hit a large steep wave on the right side (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: not available; insufficient water on the right.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: marginal; the main line remains standard.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: approaching the documented threshold - GoRafting lists Double Trouble as a high-water option above ~3,500 cfs; the AW reach calls it available at higher flows.

relative_position.notes: Above ~3,500 cfs, enter left of the first island by punching two small stacked holes (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: dry or unrunnable.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: not in play; use the right channel of the island.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: close to forming; documented as an option above ~3,500 cfs (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: A rock pile that is a known sieve sits in the center of the flow at the runout; swimmers should escape to river left (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: most exposed; swimmers must exit left immediately.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: covered but straining current; the swimmer escape route is river left (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: submerged with a strong draw; keep all swimmers left of center.

relative_position.notes: A medium-size eddy on river right below the final drop; catch it to set safety (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small but easy to catch.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard safety-set eddy for raft trains (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: faster eddy line; still the designated safety and recovery station upstream of the runout sieve.

relative_position.notes: About 0.07 mi below Troublemaker (mile ~5.18), a group of boulders with a small hump and pourover over a submerged boulder containing a foot-entrapment crack (AW reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: hump and pourover most defined; the entrapment crack is shallowest and most dangerous to standing swimmers.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: pourover active; American Whitewater documents fatal and non-fatal entrapments here.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: washed out at the surface, but the crack remains; swimmers from Troublemaker must be recovered before this point.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Documented fatal and non-fatal foot entrapments (AW reach); treat as a swimmer-recovery deadline for the rapid.

rivers.display_name: Colorado River: Lees Ferry to Pearce Ferry
Pacuare River: Tres Equis to Siquirres
Zambezi River: Boiling Pot to Mukuni Beach
Futaleufu River: Rio Azul Swinging Bridge to The Pasarela
Chilko River: Chilko River Lodge to Chilko-Taseko Junction

rapids.name: Lava Canyon
rapids.class: III-V (flow-dependent reach, exact feature scope pending)
